title: Microcontroller device-family register specification
description: Vendor-agnostic schema for microcontroller device-family register-map and peripheral register/field metadata.

spec-version.description: Spec format version using semantic versioning (semver), e.g. '1.0.0'. Increment the patch version for corrections, the minor version for backward-compatible additions, and the major version for breaking changes.

sub-families.description: List of sub-family identifiers that belong to this family.

arch-ref.description: Identifier of the architecture-level spec file (without the .yml extension) in docs/specs/common/arch/, e.g. 'cortex-m0plus'. Tooling can merge the referenced architecture spec with this family spec when generating code or documentation.

definitions.description: Reusable definitions. Values are referenced via YAML anchors/aliases in the spec.

settings.description: Named settings maps, each referenced by one or more fields.

peripherals.description: List of peripheral blocks documented in this spec.

hex-address.description: A hex address or byte offset (e.g. 0x40000000). Written in hex notation in the source YAML; parsed as an integer by most YAML parsers.

references.description: Cross-references into the source reference manual.

section.description: Single section reference (used by individual registers).

memory-region.description: A named address range in the memory map.

base.description: Peripheral base address for register offset calculations, when it differs from the region's start address. Consumers must use this address (not start) when computing register addresses from offsets.

verified.description: Whether this entry has been verified against the reference manual. Defaults to true. Set to false to flag data that is provisional or still needs checking.

settings.description: Enumerated bit-pattern settings for a field. Keys are binary bit patterns (e.g. '00', '10', '1111'). Null means no settings are documented.

field.description: A named bit-field within a register.

name.description: Field name as it appears in the reference manual.

width.description: Field width in bits (must equal msb - lsb + 1).

access.description: Register access type: rw = read/write, ro = read-only, wo = write-only.

note.description: Optional free-text annotation for this field.

priority-over.description: Names of fields that this field overrides when both are written in the same register access. If this field and any listed field are both written with a non-zero value simultaneously, the effect of this field takes precedence and the listed fields' writes are ignored by the hardware.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of when/why this sequence is required, and any caller arguments it accepts (e.g. 'write-arg' values).

steps.description: Ordered list of steps. Steps must be executed in the order listed.

sequence-step.description: A single step in a register access sequence.

op.description: Operation type: 'write' writes field values to the register; 'read' reads the register.

note.description: Human-readable explanation of this step's purpose, including any timing or ordering constraints.

optional.description: Whether this step may be omitted. Defaults to false. Optional steps should still be executed when possible.

fields.description: Field values to write. Keys are field names (or logical field groups). Values are either a non-negative integer literal (fixed value) or the string 'write-arg' (caller-supplied value). Only valid when op is 'write'.

expect.description: Expected field values to verify after reading. Keys are field names; values are the expected non-negative integers representing the field's bit pattern. A consumer should check these values and treat a mismatch as a sequence failure. Omit on read steps where the result is intentionally discarded. Only valid when op is 'read'.

offset.description: Register byte offset from the peripheral base address.

fields.description: Ordered list of bit-fields from MSB to LSB.

instances.description: Concrete instances of this peripheral in the device memory map, each binding the register layout to a named base address.

registers.description: Ordered list of register definitions. Each item is a single-key mapping whose key is the register name.

peripheral-instance.description: A named instance of a peripheral at a specific base address.

name.description: Instance name as it appears in the reference manual, e.g. 'GPIOA', 'TIM2'.

base.description: Base address of this peripheral instance.

sub-families.description: Sub-families for which this instance is present. Omit if the instance is present in all sub-families.

register-item.description: A single-key mapping where the key is the register name and the value is the register definition.

peripheral-item.description: A single-key mapping where the key is the peripheral name and the value is the peripheral definition.
